title: Application   of Immobilized   Arsenazo   III   for   Sorption-Spectroscopic Determination of Mercury
author: M.S. Bobomurodova
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The article discusses the credibility of using Arsenazo III immobilized on polyacrylonitrile fiber modified with polyethylene polyamine (PPA) as a reagent for the detection of mercury ions. The optimal conditions for immobilization and complexation have been found. This is due to its high prevalence and stability in the environment, the ability to migrate and bioconcentrate, the high toxicity of most of its compounds
steps_raw: The article discusses the credibility of using Arsenazo III immobilized on polyacrylonitrile fiber modified with polyethylene polyamine (PPA) as a reagent for the detection of mercury ions. The optimal conditions for immobilization and complexation have been found. This is due to its high prevalence and stability in the environment, the ability to migrate and bioconcentrate, the high toxicity of most of its compounds
keywords: Arsenazo   III,   fiber,   polyethylene   polyamine,   mercury   ions,   optimal   conditions, immobilization, complexation, mercury (II), natural waters, waste waters, toxicant, environment, MAC
